title: Picea homolepis
taxonomy: Picea homolepis is classified within the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the division Pinophyta (conifers), class Pinopsida, and order Pinales. It belongs to the family Pinaceae and is a member of the genus Picea. Picea homolepis is a species of spruce native to Japan. It is closely related to other Picea species, which are found in various parts of the Northern Hemisphere. The classification of Picea homolepis helps to understand its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
uses: Picea homolepis, also known as Japanese black spruce, is a lesser-known species with limited research on its medicinal properties. intro: Picea homolepis, also known as Japanese white spruce, is a coniferous evergreen tree native to Japan and Korea. The health benefits of Picea homolepis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
